jokes.text: Are Bees Building Hives in your Diaper Genie? The Deadly Truth about Oxygen.
jokes.setup: Marge reads magazine headlines that should be helpful parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fear-mongering about everyday items, including oxygen itself
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of parenting magazine hysteria with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Growing up means giving up everything that makes you happy. It's true. I've given up everything but raisins.
jokes.setup: Marge tries to comfort Maggie about losing her pacifier
jokes.punchline: Her 'wisdom' is deeply depressing and specific about her own sad compromises
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge characterization - trying to be wise but accidentally revealing her own depression

jokes.text: And the doctor says they're killing me. Sweet, plump coffin nails is what they are.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions she only has raisins left
jokes.punchline: Even her one remaining pleasure is literally killing her, described poetically
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Marge's despair with unexpected poetic phrasing

jokes.text: It's a Larry Flynt publication! Lisa, stop reading mastheads. I can't. I won't!
jokes.setup: Homer reveals the parenting magazine is from a porn publisher
jokes.punchline: Lisa's compulsive need to read mastheads becomes dramatic defiance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lisa's obsessive nature, decent escalation

jokes.text: How about Syntho-Nip? Kiddy Cork? Thumbstitute? AccuTeat? Bink 182? Want to suck on the receipt?
jokes.setup: Homer brings various pacifier brands for Maggie
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd brand names culminating in offering a receipt
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay escalation with good Blink-182 reference and absurd ending

jokes.text: Larry Flynt? Larry Flynt? Larry Flynt? Larry Flynt, Larry Flynt, Larry Flynt?!
jokes.setup: Baby Maggie crying inconsolably
jokes.punchline: Her cries sound exactly like she's saying 'Larry Flynt' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd callback - the idea that a baby would be repeating this specific reference is wonderfully surreal

jokes.text: Good work, boy. Just for that, we're going to treat your heart murmur, not just 'see how it goes'.
jokes.setup: Bart successfully calms Maggie
jokes.punchline: Doctor reveals he was withholding medical care based on behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark medical ethics joke delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh, stupid baby. Growing her brain on my dime.
jokes.setup: Homer is frustrated after the doctor visit
jokes.punchline: He's annoyed that his baby is developing intelligence at his expense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - viewing his child's development as a personal inconvenience

jokes.text: Nappien activates your brain's napping centers, and attacks your body's awake-agens. And unlike Sleepia, it won't cause foot fattening or elbow stink.
jokes.setup: Pharmaceutical commercial for sleep aid
jokes.punchline: Made-up medical terms and ridiculous side effects
jokes.explanation: Good pharmaceutical parody with absurd made-up terms

jokes.text: Thank you, methasorbizone tartrate, also known as Nappien.
jokes.setup: Homer wakes up refreshed from the sleep medication
jokes.punchline: He formally thanks the drug by its ridiculous chemical name
jokes.explanation: Homer's formal gratitude to a made-up drug name is mildly amusing

jokes.text: And someone used our videotapes as dominoes.
jokes.setup: Marge lists Homer's sleep-walking destruction
jokes.punchline: Absurd misuse of household items for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity but not particularly strong

jokes.text: If I had set up those dominoes, I'd be wearing my special domino-setting-up kneepads.
jokes.setup: Homer denies setting up videotapes as dominoes
jokes.punchline: His 'proof' reveals he owns specialized equipment for this exact activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - his denial accidentally proves he does this regularly

jokes.text: Mood swings?! Mood swings. Mood swings! Mood swings? Mood swings! Mood swings!
jokes.setup: Marge reads side effects including mood swings
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately demonstrates mood swings through varying delivery
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective demonstration of the side effect

jokes.text: So you're the one behind all these hijinks at the Rock 'N' Roll Wax Museum.
jokes.setup: Homer sleep-walks and ends up at a wax museum
jokes.punchline: Guard treats it as a serious crime against wax figures
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag setup but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Well, son, your Uncle Homer is like 80% of America wacked out on prescription drugs.
jokes.setup: Police officer asks why Homer hurt the wax figures
jokes.punchline: Homer casually cites a statistic about prescription drug abuse
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh! I have three kids and no money! Why can't I have no kids and three money?
jokes.setup: Homer explains he's stressed
jokes.punchline: Mangled grammar creates absurd inverse wish
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons quote - perfect Homer grammar error that creates brilliant absurd math

jokes.text: Want to come out and play? Zombie kill. No, play! Zombie file grievance.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to get zombie Homer to play
jokes.punchline: Zombie Homer's bureaucratic response to being corrected
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of zombie tropes with workplace bureaucracy

jokes.text: Zombie montage.
jokes.setup: Kids are about to do various activities with zombie Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart announces the upcoming montage sequence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective meta-humor breaking the fourth wall

jokes.text: Zombie montage.
jokes.setup: Bart about to show off zombie Homer's capabilities
jokes.punchline: Announces the upcoming montage sequence directly
jokes.explanation: Meta moment acknowledging TV tropes - economical and self-aware without being overly clever

jokes.text: What are you, a travel agent? 'Cause you're sending me on a guilt trip.
jokes.setup: Someone tells Homer he's horribly injured the fire department
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun deflecting guilt with travel metaphor
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy dad joke pun, which fits Homer's character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, not as bad as knowing that somewhere, gays are marrying each other. That's the real emergency, Kent.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks burning man how it feels knowing no one will save him
jokes.punchline: He's more concerned about gay marriage than his own imminent death
jokes.explanation: Dark social satire about misplaced priorities, typical Simpsons commentary

jokes.text: Just call me Fireman Skinner.
jokes.setup: Skinner volunteering to be a firefighter
jokes.punchline: His earnest, formal way of announcing his new identity
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Skinner's formality makes the simple statement slightly amusing

jokes.text: Lousy civilians. I wish I could burn 'em all.
jokes.setup: Moe becomes Fire Chief and turns away volunteers
jokes.punchline: Immediately expresses desire to burn the people he's supposed to protect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - immediately corrupted by tiny amount of authority

jokes.text: Is that one? That's just someone barbecuing. Oh! Is that one? That's a guy with red hair.
jokes.setup: Homer eagerly looking for fires to fight
jokes.punchline: Mistaking increasingly non-fire things for emergencies
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overeagerness - the red hair mistake shows his simple pattern recognition

jokes.text: Is that one? That's just someone barbecuing. Oh! Is that one? That's a guy with red hair.
jokes.setup: Homer eagerly looks for fires to fight
jokes.punchline: His desperation leads to mistaking increasingly mundane things for fires
jokes.explanation: Simple escalating misidentification gag, moderately effective

jokes.text: Soggy, smoky hero.
jokes.setup: Homer describing how he'll look after firefighting
jokes.punchline: Unappetizing description that sounds like food
jokes.explanation: Alliterative phrase that makes heroism sound unappetizing - economical wordplay

jokes.text: Luigi give you all the food you want! On the house!
jokes.setup: Luigi thanks the firemen for saving his restaurant
jokes.punchline: Offers free food at a restaurant that just burned down
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about offering food from a burned restaurant

jokes.text: You save-a Luigi's place! Mwah! Mwah! Luigi give you all the food you want! On the house!
jokes.setup: Luigi thanking the firefighters
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical Italian restaurant owner offering free food
jokes.explanation: Standard ethnic stereotype humor - functional but relies on clichés

jokes.text: Hey, this is sweet, huh? Almost makes up for not gettin' paid. We don't get what?
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying the perks of being thanked for firefighting
jokes.punchline: Apu's shock reveals Homer didn't understand volunteer meant unpaid
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's misunderstanding of basic concepts like 'volunteer'

jokes.text: Frankenberry the Movie 2: The Frankenberry Wears Prada
jokes.setup: Movie star gives crew jackets from failed films
jokes.punchline: Absurd cereal character movie sequel with fashion movie title mashup
jokes.explanation: Silly movie title mashup, moderately clever but not exceptional

jokes.text: People owe us because we're heroes. It's even on my business card. I printed them on the back of my old business cards. See? It's different.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why they deserve compensation
jokes.punchline: His 'proof' is cheaply made business cards that contradict professionalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - his cheap, half-hearted attempt at professionalism while claiming heroic status

jokes.text: So, I see by your tie you're a Yale man, too. Let's croon, shall we? Boola, boola, boola, boola...
jokes.setup: Burns is about to jump into a rescue net
jokes.punchline: He stops to bond over Ivy League credentials and sing fight songs
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment - even in danger, he prioritizes class solidarity

jokes.text: But since I thought you were dead, you won't get paid this week. Make a note of it.
jokes.setup: Burns realizes Smithers survived the fire
jokes.punchline: Uses the mistaken death as excuse to dock pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic - finding a way to profit from employee's near-death experience

jokes.text: Go back to your tenements, where the O'Briens live next to the Goldbergs, who rub elbows with Antonellis, and the only thing you have in common is the squalor of your chamber pot!
jokes.setup: Burns dismisses the volunteer firemen without reward
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, antiquated insult about immigrant tenement living
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns characterization with period-appropriate classist rant

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. It ain't stealing if you take it fast.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests they might be stealing from fire victims
jokes.punchline: Moe's ridiculous legal theory based on speed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe logic - criminal behavior justified by completely irrelevant technicality

jokes.text: And some stuff I wanted.
jokes.setup: Homer listing 'vaporized' merchandise to store owner
jokes.punchline: Casually admits personal motivation for the fake inventory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty - casually admits his criminal intent with childlike directness

jokes.text: It would be a shame if the next fire you fought was in hell!
jokes.setup: Homer considers reporting their theft
jokes.punchline: Moe threatens him with supernatural consequences
jokes.explanation: Good character threat from Moe mixing gangster intimidation with religious imagery

jokes.text: It would be a shame if the next fire you fought was in hell!
jokes.setup: Moe threatening Apu for considering reporting them
jokes.punchline: Overwrought threat that's both dark and absurd
jokes.explanation: Good Moe threat - combines his criminal tendencies with firefighter context in appropriately over-the-top way

jokes.text: Oh, thank God there's a fire. Marge's birthday's coming up.
jokes.setup: Fire alarm going off
jokes.punchline: Homer excited about fire because it means stealing opportunities for gift
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer moment - his complete moral corruption expressed through twisted romantic gesture

jokes.text: Oh, bogus gizmo, grant me eternal life.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying stolen Ionic Breeze air purifier
jokes.punchline: Treats cheap gadget like a magical artifact
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his simple faith in technology mixed with self-aware dismissal of it as 'bogus'

jokes.text: Look at me, guys! I'm stealing five Segways at once! And speaking of segues, I'm off to my next morally questionable activity!
jokes.setup: Homer showing off multiple stolen Segways
jokes.punchline: Pun on Segway/segue while transitioning to next crime
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works both as pun and transition device - Homer's awareness of the pun makes it funnier

jokes.text: We're not thieves; we're scavengers, like the beautiful vulture or the heroic tapeworm, or America's sweetheart, the maggot.
jokes.setup: Homer defends their theft with animal comparisons
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting creatures described with positive adjectives
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with perfect final punchline about 'America's sweetheart, the maggot'

jokes.text: Oh! This thing's hotter than Ellen Barkin!
jokes.setup: Moe grabs a hot grandfather clock in a fire
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate celebrity comparison while in mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Dated celebrity reference delivered at inappropriate moment, typical Moe

jokes.text: But it's been in my family for over 40 seconds!
jokes.setup: Homer tells Moe to let go of the stolen clock to save his life
jokes.punchline: Moe claims sentimental attachment to recently stolen item
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of family heirloom sentiment - claiming deep attachment to something just stolen

jokes.text: Apu is dead. I was reincarnated as this cat.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about Nabu (Apu's cat)
jokes.punchline: Apu claims to have died and been reincarnated as his own pet
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment playing with reincarnation beliefs

jokes.text: Oh, you have just been Apu'd!
jokes.setup: Apu successfully tricks Homer with fake death story
jokes.punchline: Apu coins a phrase for being deceived by him
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor with character creating catchphrase for himself

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm the bum of the future!
jokes.setup: Homer explains they sent stolen goods to help the homeless
jokes.punchline: Homeless person excited about high-tech stolen goods
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about technology gap, moderately effective ending
